name: Basic Recipe--Cheesecake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 325°F.
2. Position rack in the center of the oven.
3. Butter a 9-inch springform pan.
4. Wrap the outside of the pan with a double layer of heavy-duty foil.
5. Press the crust firmly into the bottom and 1 inch up the sides of the pan; refrigerate until needed or bake and cool.
6. With mixer on high speed, beat cream cheese until smooth, about 5 minutes.
7. Combine sugar and flour. Reduce speed to low; beat sugar mixture into cream cheese, 1/4 cup at a time, until smooth, about 3 minutes.
8. In a bowl, stir together eggs, sour cream, heavy cream, yolks, and vanilla until smooth.
9. Gradually beat egg mixture into cheese mixture until just incorporated.
10. Pour batter into the pan.
11. Place the pan in a large baking pan on the oven rack; fill the baking pan with water halfway up the sides of the springform pan.
12. Bake for 1 1/2 hours or until the center of the cheesecake is set.
13. Remove from water bath; remove foil.
14. Cool on a wire rack for 30 minutes; cool completely in the refrigerator about 3 hours, or overnight.
- Serving size: 16 servings
- Per serving (including crust): 401 calories; 7 grams protein; 28 grams fat; 156 milligrams cholesterol; 32 grams carbohydrates; 293 milligrams sodium
- Completed cheesecakes in series: 400-500 calories; 7-9 grams protein; 27-37 grams fat; 151-163 milligrams cholesterol; 23-43 grams carbohydrates; 250-300 milligrams sodium
- Your time in the kitchen: 30 minutes; ready to serve in 5 1/2 hours.
- Notes: These cheesecakes can be frozen, without toppings, for up to two months. Wrap in plastic wrap and foil to prevent freezer burn. Let thaw, wrapped, in the refrigerator overnight.
